intro: Ponthieva koehleri, also known as Koehler's Ponthieva, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to Central America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ponthieva koehleri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ponthieva koehleri is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infusion, and is used to make teas, syrups, and topical creams to harness its therapeutic benefits.
